title: Fiat mafia PSD, pereat mundus
summary: The article analyzes PSD's campaign to remove Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan from office, warning that political destabilization could lead to loss of EU funds, credit rating downgrades, and the rise of extremism. The author examines possible political scenarios including a no-confidence motion supported by AUR, and criticizes President Nicușor Dan for his vague stance on the crisis.
talking_points: PSD's obsessive campaign to remove PM Bolojan threatens Romania's financial stability and EU fund absorption of 10 billion euros in grants due by August
AUR plans to file a no-confidence motion in late April, potentially giving PSD cover to vote against its own government
PSD faces a strategic dilemma: governing with AUR contradicts their stated principles, while early elections would be devastating given their declining poll numbers around 10-16%
President Nicușor Dan's vague responses raise fears he may capitulate to PSD demands, potentially sacrificing USR from the governing coalition
The author warns that political instability could trigger credit rating downgrades, erode public trust, and open the door to soft totalitarian extremism
author: Rodica Culcer
